programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: The prime suspect in a serial killer case dies, and his spirit makes a pact with Allison, suggesting that he will lead her to the real culprit if she will agree to deliver a message to his girlfriend. Supernatural drama, starring Patricia Arquette(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Drama set in a fictional world where black people rule over a white underclass, following two young people who are divided by their race but united by love. Callum McGregor is angered when his friend is attacked by police officers during a night out but his parents tell him to do nothing, otherwise he may jeopardise his entry into an elite military academy. But an unexpected reunion with old school friend Sephy, a member of the ruling class, ignites a spark that both of them find difficult to ignore. Masali Baduza and Jack Rowan star, with Helen Baxendale, Paterson Joseph and Shaun Dingwall. Based on the novel by Malorie Blackman(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook

Cooking against the clock, they are paired up with chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid and produce dishes from around the world on a budget of £5, from squid tempura to pad Thai and Korean chicken wings. Plus, the 10-minute challenge throws up cornflakes and ginger nut biscuits. Hosted by Rylan Clarke-Neal(n)

programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the final of the dancing contest. Just four performers remain in the contest, and with the help of the dance coaches Cheryl, Matthew Morrison, Oti Mabuse and Todrick Hall, they will put on one last show to impress the studio audience and win £50,000 and a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Who will become The Greatest Dancer? Plus, another world class guest artist performs a unique collaboration(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Prue Leith, Brian Turner, Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Hala El-Shafie, Ed Baines, Michael Caines and Anna Haugh(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as cricketer Graeme Swann and actress Rachel Parris take on Olympic hockey player Sam Quek and comedian Mark Watson(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby is back at work but she is losing patience with her mother, Lavender, and has to choose between her career and parenthood. Elsewhere, Megan offers Archie a job with her in Sudan, but she is not sure if she wants to leave, and Jade finally loses her patience with Marty when he is late for another shift(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from Woking Recycling Centre in Surrey, including a retro two-seater sofa and a vintage sewing box(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Cousins Joe and Alex, who have both experienced sight-loss problems, compete in the culinary contest. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Paignton, south Devon, where the Dartmouth Steam Railway takes him to the home of Agatha Christie, in the company of her great-grandson James Prichard(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Plus, reports from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in St Albans - a converted farmyard barn with a modern twist, a zero-carbon self-build and a 1950s Scandinavian-style bungalow(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey around the nation, with a visit to Evesham in Worcestershire, where they sample a pork pie wedding cake, meet a plum charmer, dine in one of Britain's oldest inns, and attend an asparagus festival(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Though under pressure, Tom makes time for Patricia. Rory seeks leverage on a man withholding vital evidence, while Sean is hurt by a confession(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: Three more amateur designers take up the challenge as they each attempt to transform a bedroom in a thatched cottage in the village of Briantspuddle in Dorset, with three days, £1,000 and a small team of assistants to help deliver their projects. Meanwhile, Tom Dyckhoff examines the history of the community, which was the result of an unusual Edwardian experiment(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through the National Archives, unearthing the original letter that gave birth to Jack the Ripper, examining the mysterious disappearance of a wartime hero, and exploring the secret UFO files kept by the Ministry of Defence(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Michael Baggott assess objects brought into the University of Nottingham, while presenter Paul Martin learns about a Nottingham-born Victorian prize-fighter called Bendigo and meets locals who are keeping his memory alive(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Glasgow to Cumbrae, learning about a forgotten Scottish expedition to the Antarctic and how one woman led a protest against high rents in the city(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Clovelly in Devon, looking at its transformation from fishing harbour to romantic Victorian seaside resort. Thanks to a powerful and pioneering woman, Clovelly found itself at the forefront of the holiday revolution, and even today cars are banned and the village is privately owned, helping to preserve it as a reminder of a bygone age(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria and Albert curator Keith brings the original Frankenstein's monster out of the archives for a sci-fi exhibition, including the clothes worn by actor Boris Karloff in the 1935 horror classic Bride of Frankenstein. But it's clear the figure has seen better days and will need plenty of work to bring it back to life. Meanwhile, the curators discover photographs of the real Alice in Wonderland and repairs are made to Shakespeare's First Folio(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on the work of staff at Alder Hey Children's Hospital. Eight-month-old Elsie was not expected to survive being born due to serious kidney abnormalities. But she has spent all but two weeks of her life in hospital and has 11 operations to repair the damage caused by a genetic problem. Now she faces surgery yet again. Holly has cerebral palsy and, as she is about to turn 18, is preparing to transition to adult services in Liverpool - a huge leap for her parents who have grown to depend on the hospital's care(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Comedy series with Vic Reeves and Bob Mortimer, featuring a welcome return for Novelty Island, a special rendition of That's Magic, as well as an appearance from Michael Portillo as you have never seen him before(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the fifth-round tie between Derby County and Manchester United at Pride Park. Plus, a look back at Tuesday and Wednesday's matches in the competition(n)

programme.title: Live Women's International Football
programme.desc: USA v England (Kick-off 12.00am). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the opening match for both nations in the four-team round-robin tournament, held at Exploria Stadium in Orlando, Florida. England won this competition last year after drawing 2-2 with today's opponents either side of victories over Japan and Brazil. However, it was the Americans who reigned supreme in last summer's World Cup, defeating Phil Neville's side 2-1 in the semi-finals en route to retaining the trophy(n)

programme.title: Confronting Holocaust Denial with David Baddiel
programme.desc: The Holocaust is one of the most documented, witnessed and written about events in history, yet one in six people worldwide either think its scale has been exaggerated or deny that it took place. In this film, comedian David Baddiel explores the multi-faceted nature of Holocaust denial - in both historical and contemporary terms - in an attempt to understand what motivates this phenomenon and why it is on the rise, both in Britain and across the globe(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood joins the US Army's First Armoured Division, travelling into the desert to take part in a field exercise testing the combat readiness of troops and weaponry(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether yoga can lower a person's blood pressure as much as a workout, and Alain Gregoire tests meal replacement shakes to see if they are as healthy as they claim. Meanwhile, Zoe Williams discovers why wearing contact lenses in the shower damage eyesight, and Giles Yeo tests whether gourmet salts are any healthier than table salt(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades saves an old radio, a set of chairs and a side table and chest of drawers from being thrown in the tip, hoping he and two others can make money by restoring the items(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson searches for a Cambridgeshire home for a young couple with a £570,000 budget who want to escape suburban Kent. Keen to explore more of the country's wildlife, Jules also visits the Fens and finds out about the UK's first nature reserves(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams review the extraordinary events that have taken place this week as well as look back over an eventful year in the Cairngorms. With three species of Egrets now breeding in Britain, butterflies on the wing year-round and record-breaking temperatures in the Scottish Highlands, they also explores what winters may be like in the future(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Plus, reports from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to St Albans, Hertfordshire, to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into the houses before deciding which one they like the best. Narrated by Marcus Garvey(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: On a visit to Kent, Terry Wogan and Mason McQueen head to Canterbury Cathedral to learn how it helped keep hundreds fed during the Second World War. The pair also call in at a train shed that is now home to a flourishing food market, before popping across to Whitstable to sample its lesser-known delicacy of whelks. At Kent's county cricket ground Mason has a go at leather on willow, before everything stops for tea(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Paudge's vendetta against Doyle becomes physical, Kevin Dunne agonises over confiding in Ash, and Keith risks being caught out at Alice's place(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The three remaining designers work on a smart Georgian terrace in Bath, each with a living room to improve for clients demanding high expectations. One of the contestants opts for ingenious architectural furniture designs to impress, while another attempts to create a Parisian scheme on a tight budget and one struggles to restrain their usual chaotic style. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff, with judges Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood deciding which two designers will make it through to the next round(n)

programme.title: Portillo's State Secrets
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his search through the National Archives, unearthing the secret documents that show why Britain didn't boycott the Berlin Olympics of 1936. He also looks at files revealing Churchill's attempt to ban the world's first black heavyweight boxing champion, and a royal proclamation outlawing football(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items in Kent to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Host Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castle's secret tunnels.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgow's Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michael's final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands(n)

programme.title: Extreme Wales with Richard Parks
programme.desc: The endurance athlete explores Britain's deepest cave under the Brecon Beacons and attempts to overcome his fear of enclosed spaces as he spirals and squeezes though narrow gaps on a two-day subterranean adventure(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Loose Women co-hosts Nadia Sawalha and Kaye Adams join forces with experts Tim Medhurst and Anita Manning in the antique shops of Edinburgh and beyond. Nadia picks up quite the menagerie of antique animals, including three monkeys and a reindeer, while Kaye gambles a large chunk of her £400 on an art deco bracelet. An unsavoury incident at the auction and an accusation of foul play makes for a tense finale(n)

programme.title: Wild Cuba: A Caribbean Journey
programme.desc: Cuba's isolation has helped preserve many of its natural riches, creating unspoiled landscapes home to many enchanting animals. In the first of a two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson explores corners of the country that few outsiders have seen. Amongst the wonders he encounters is the bee hummingbird, the world's tiniest bird, and the spectacle of thousands of crabs migrating en masse. In this authored odyssey, the natural magic of one Earth's most intriguing countries is revealed(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Holly Walsh and Cariad Lloyd join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at multiple organisms, with questions touching on the world's deadliest hunter, a photographer hidden inside an ox, and a juggling otter. As ever, the quiz sees correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Kerry Godliman, Milton Jones, Chris Washington and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another. From November 2019(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents analysis of the day's national and international events(n)

programme.title: The Beguiled (2017)
programme.desc: The arrival of a wounded Civil War soldier brings jealousy and betrayal to a Southern girls' school. Period drama, starring Colin Farrell, Nicole Kidman and Kirsten Dunst(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of thousands of people fall victim to scams in the UK every year, with many are run from criminal call centres in India, where teams of fraudsters operate around the clock. Panorama has obtained hacked CCTV footage from inside one scam call centre that shows exactly how it works, with reporter Rajini Vaidyanathan tracking down the man behind the scam and hearing from British victims who have been conned(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Cybermen are on the march and as the last remaining humans are ruthlessly hunted down, Graham, Ryan and Yaz face a terrifying fight to survive. Civilisations fall and others rise anew, while lies are exposed, truths are revealed and battles are fought. And for the Doctor - trapped and alone - nothing will ever be the same again.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Radio host Mark Wright and CBBC vlogger Lauren Layfield enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Lindsey Russell and Richie Driss celebrate World Book Day by announcing the winners of this year's Blue Peter Book Awards and also reveal who will be crowned the best of the past 20 years as voted for by viewers. Plus, a performance by the cast of Six the Musical(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads into the Australian outback to fulfil a lifelong dream of finding the most venomous snake in the world, before hunting for the blue-ringed octopus in Sydney(n)

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion, that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth(n)

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: Secrets of the Furious Five
programme.desc: Animated semi-sequel to Kung Fu Panda in which the history of the fearless defenders of the Valley of Peace is revealed as they perform thrilling feats of bravery. Dragon warrior Po (Jack Black) faces his greatest challenge - teaching a class of novice kung fu students. With the voices of Dustin Hoffman, David Cross, Carol Kane and Jaycee Chan(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The natural history documentary follows the work of tough Australian Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to rescuing and raising orphaned kangaroos in the outback near Alice Springs. This two-part film follows Brolga as he expands operations by opening his doors to a whole new bunch of animals, including a trio of camels, a southern hairy-nosed wombat and three emu chicks, as well as building a wildlife hospital(n)

programme.title: Lorraine's Fast, Fresh and Easy Food
programme.desc: Lorraine Pascale shows how to cook a range of straightforward and satisfying dishes while juggling a hectic daily routine, and offers tips on making starters, main courses and desserts that are ideal for sharing, as well as quick family meals(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore how menus at Britain's pub have changed dramatically from the days when only nuts and pork scratchings were available for customers. They re-create dishes that would make any self-respecting publican proud, and also try their hand at making cider the traditional way(n)

programme.title: Sport Relief 2019
programme.desc: Comedy star Jennifer Saunders and Strictly Come Dancing professional Oti Mabuse captain the teams in this charity match in aid of Sport Relief. Clare Balding presents the action as players including sports stars Denise Lewis, Katherine Grainger, Jade Jones and Katherine Merry, actresses Sarah Hadland and Sally Phillips, presenters Vick Hope, Ellie Taylor and Lindsey Russell and Love Island's Montana Brown take to the court(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: Tricks of the Trade
programme.desc: Architects Piers Taylor and Gurmeet Sian help two homeowners in need of life-changing transformations to their existing homes. In Sutton Coldfield, West Midlands, they come to the aid of builder Lee, who added an extension to his suburban semi. However, two years later the ground floor is half built, and his partner Jaimelee and their three children are left living in a building site. Meanwhile, Suzanne, Antonio and toddler Sophia struggle with the awkward layout of their west London flat, giving Gurmeet the challenge of rethinking it(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall goes in search of wildlife in the West Country and meets some of the region's most dedicated nature lovers, beginning in the Wye Valley and the Forest of Dean. He catches minnows in the River Wye, gets an insight into the life of Britain's only aquatic songbird and meets an IT technician who has set up miniature cameras in his garden to record animals(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The continued work of Australian naturalist Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to raising kangaroos and is now opening his doors to new animals. However, it is not proving easy. The emu chicks are wreaking havoc, the camels are rapidly outgrowing their enclosure and the wombat is proving quite a handful. To help, Brolga is moving some of them to his 80-acre sanctuary - but after a clash with Roger the kangaroo, he has been left on crutches, and all his plans are on hold(n)

programme.title: Greg James' Sport Relief Heroes
programme.desc: A defining feature of Sport Relief is the willingness of celebrities to undertake gruelling challenges in order to inspire the public to donate and get involved with the cause. Greg looks back with celebs including David Walliams, Eddie Izzard, Davina McCall, John Bishop and Zoe Ball, all of whom have put themselves to the test and beyond for the charity, finding out what motivated them(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. In the final episode, the Royal National Lifeboat Institution volunteers race to rescue two teenagers stuck in mud in Cleethorpes on the Humber estuary before the incoming tide overwhelms them. Elsewhere, a team in Devon help a crew of a yacht shipwrecked on a notorious sand bar, and a routine call to pick up a boat that has broken its moorings becomes a race to save a family of four(n)

programme.title: Wild Cuba: A Caribbean Journey
programme.desc: Cuba's isolation has helped preserve many of its natural riches, creating unspoiled landscapes home to many enchanting animals. In the first of a two-part series, cameraman, director and naturalist Colin Stafford-Johnson explores corners of the country that few outsiders have seen. Amongst the wonders he encounters is the bee hummingbird, the world's tiniest bird, and the spectacle of thousands of crabs migrating en masse. In this authored odyssey, the natural magic of one Earth's most intriguing countries is revealed(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey to Quebec City, beginning by boarding the train from Nova Scotia to New Brunswick. Along the way he investigates the world's biggest tide at Hopewell Rocks and admires its dramatic rock formations and caves. He also appears to defy gravity on a magnetic hill in a 1965 Pontiac Bonneville, before joining the Elsipogtog First Nation to learn about quilting and traditional dress. He also goes fishing for oysters and helps quarry 150-year-old sandstone(n)

programme.title: Hilary Mantel: Return to Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Profile of author Hilary Mantel in the six months leading up to the publication of The Mirror and the Light, the final novel in her award-winning Tudor trilogy telling the story of the extraordinary rise and sudden fall of Thomas Cromwell. Hilary reveals why she embarked on the writing of the trilogy and how it has changed her life. She also delves into her past and how she grew up with a dark family secret(n)

programme.title: Lost Lives (2019)
programme.desc: A documentary inspired by the book of the same name that records the circumstances of the deaths of every man, woman and child killed during the Troubles. With extracts read by stars of stage and screen including Roma Downey, Liam Neeson, Adrian Dunbar, Michelle Fairley, Ciaran Hinds, Bronagh Gallagher, Brendan Gleeson, Susan Lynch, Stephen Rea and Kenneth Branagh(n)

programme.title: Midnight's Children (2012)
programme.desc: Two babies are born at midnight on the day India becomes independent of British rule in 1947, but are switched at birth. As the two children grow to adulthood, one in wealth the other in poverty, they find their lives are constantly intertwined while the nation around them experiences sweeping social change. Drama based on Salman Rushdie's novel, starring Satya Bhabha and Siddharth(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer Paul Rose's trip concludes in Wharfedale, where he talks to some of the women whose story inspired the film Calendar Girls, finds out why bats are under threat in the area and meets families from inner-city Bradford who are having their first Dales experience(n)

programme.title: The Real T-Rex with Chris Packham
programme.desc: Chris Packham embarks on a global journey to discover the truth about the tyrannosaurus rex, which has endured centuries of scientific inaccuracy and Hollywood misrepresentation. The naturalist uses new studies into dinosaur skin, teeth and muscles, as well as groundbreaking insights into its behaviour to create the most accurate CGI representation of the dinosaur ever produced. To help, Chris speaks to experts in birds, biomechanics and alligators, and has access to one of the most complete tyrannosaurus rex skeletons in existence(n)

programme.title: The Day the Dinosaurs Died
programme.desc: Documentary examining whether an asteroid strike 66 million years ago triggered a worldwide catastrophe, killing off the dinosaurs and wiping out 75 per cent of all life on Earth. Evolutionary biologist Ben Garrod joins a multimillion pound drilling expedition into the exact spot in the Gulf of Mexico that was the point of impact, while palaeopathologist Alice Roberts meets leading scientists to find if the effects could have wiped out dinosaurs across the world almost immediately(n)

programme.title: The Stuarts
programme.desc: Clare Jackson examines how the Stuarts became fatally divided by religion, with brothers in opposition and two daughters supporting the overthrow of their father. After the Catholic James II was deposed by the Protestant William of Orange in 1688, Scotland lost its sovereignty and became part of Great Britain in 1707, while Ireland was reduced from a kingdom to a colony(n)

programme.title: Female Filmmakers: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez introduces a selection of short films all made by women, telling stories from a contemporary female perspective(n)

programme.title: How We Built Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby completes his journey through Britain, tracing the arrival of the bold new modernist style at a glorious public swimming pool on the south coast. After assessing the impact of the Second World War on architecture, he travels to the capital and asks how many of the breathtaking towers of steel and glass will still be standing in 100 years from now(n)

programme.title: Rhythms of India
programme.desc: Soumik Datta's musical journey around India ends with a look at the popular music scene, discovering how Bollywood is changing, the impact of the internet and the rise of hip hop. He starts in Mumbai, where screenwriter Javed Akhtar describes the roots of popular songs in Bollywood's storytelling tradition(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Cameras venture inside places of sporting achievement, scientific endeavour and male obsession - the lofts of pigeon fanciers - to tell the story of a remarkable bird. As racer, messenger and even beauty pageant contestant, the humble pigeon has been a steadfast part of British life for centuries(n)

programme.title: Age of the Image
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox explores how the power of images has transformed the modern world, beginning with the explosion of scientific and technological advances at the start of the 20th century that created radical new ways of looking at the world(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Sybil Ruscoe host the edition first shown on April 20, featuring performances by Transvision Vamp, Inner City, Holly Johnson, Metallica, Midnight Oil, the Cure, Simple Minds, the Bangles and the Beatmasters with Merlin(n)

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first shown on April 27, featuring performances by London Boys, Natalie Cole, De la Soul, Fine Young Cannibals, Morrissey, the Beatmasters with Merlin, Yazz, the Bangles and Poison(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Carly Simon discusses her life and career, reflecting on her time with ex-husband James Taylor, a heroin addict when they first met. She also recounts her number of highly public affairs in the early 1970s and how her experience fed into the 1972 album's most famous song, You're So Vain, performing the missing fourth verse on the piano - the first time she has ever sung it along with the melody. Plus, footage of Simon and Taylor Swift performing You're So Vain together, and extracts from an interview where Swift talks about her love for the song(n)

programme.title: Guitar, Drum and Bass
programme.desc: The American musician and singer-songwriter of Talking Heads and Tom Tom Club fame celebrates the contribution of bass to popular music, tracing its progress from street corner doo wop and the overlooked 'guy at the back' in rock 'n' roll via Paul McCartney, James Jamerson and Carol Kaye. She reflects on the emergence of '70s funky bass stars Bootsy Collins and Chic's Bernard Edwards, the driving lead bass of postpunk maverick Peter Hook in both Joy Division and New Order through to the growth of Bass Culture in reggae, whose sound systems sparked new genres in drum 'n' bass, grime and beyond(n)

programme.title: Michael Kiwanuka at the 6 Music Festival
programme.desc: A set by the singer-songwriter, from the Roundhouse, north London, whose brand of folk-inflected soul has been likened to that of Bill Withers, Otis Redding and Terry Callie(n)

programme.title: Girl in a Band: Tales from the Rock 'n' Roll Front Line
programme.desc: Journalist Kate Mossman tries to look beyond the cliches of fallen angels, grunge babes and rock chicks as she gets the untold stories from rock's front line to discover if it has always been different for female members in a band. Contributors include Elkie Brooks and Carol Kaye(n)

programme.title: Cilla at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of memorable TV appearances by Cilla Black from the BBC archives. The much-missed performer began her presenting career in 1968 on the BBC, having already become one of Britain's most popular singers. The programme features her appearances on Not Only, But Also, The Ken Dodd Show, Top of the Pops and The Royal Variety Performance as well as her self-titled series, plus her famous 1973 duet with Marc Bolan(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: Carly Simon discusses her life and career, reflecting on her time with ex-husband James Taylor, a heroin addict when they first met. She also recounts her number of highly public affairs in the early 1970s and how her experience fed into the 1972 album's most famous song, You're So Vain, performing the missing fourth verse on the piano - the first time she has ever sung it along with the melody. Plus, footage of Simon and Taylor Swift performing You're So Vain together, and extracts from an interview where Swift talks about her love for the song(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on another round of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, beginning with Portuguese capital Lisbon. The culinary effects of Portugal's historic explorations are still seen in its dishes with hints of cinnamon and freshly chopped coriander from the east and tomatoes and chillies from the Americas. Rick is inspired to cook salt cod fritters and an almond tart, before heading for the St Anthony's Day Parade and Sardine Festival(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: An exploration of the effects of the eruption of volcanos in the ocean. From these violent beginnings emerge islands and coral reefs that are home to a vast array of wildlife, including giant manta rays and vampire bugs that thrive in the tropical snow, after a process that takes millions of years. Benedict Cumberbatch narrates(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Karl Lewis admits while questioned by Cadi that the accusations he brought against Ellis were false. Cadi turns her attention to Mia, whose false testimony led to Karl's arrest. Crime drama, starring Sian Reese-Williams. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Miami
programme.desc: Clive sets out to discover the truth about an American city that was seen as the vice capital of America, teeming with money, illegal aliens and cocaine. He investigates what is beneath the surface, meeting famous resdients in Miami Vice star Don Johnson and singer Gloria Estefan(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Chicago
programme.desc: The writer visits the Windy City and finds there is a lot more to Chicago than memories of Al Capone and the St Valentine's Day Massacre. The people are surprisingly forthright about the problems of living in a city where millions of dollars change hands at the board of trade, but little is done to solve the problems of gang violence in the housing projects where shootings are a daily event(n)

programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold are celebrating another noisy victory, gleefully ringing Big Ben's bell when an over-excited Penfold falls, pushing the clock hand forward, causing the sun to go down prematurely. programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie is relaxing at home, playing games and watching television. She shows us how a television screen works using millions of tiny squares called pixels. Using her special microscope camera, Maddie shows us how a pixel can make any colour from just three coloured lights - red, blue and green. Next she visits a factory where carpet is made. Maddie follows the journey that wool takes, from shearing a sheep to going through the factory, to end up in the carpets we have at home. Plus, a housing inspection in Liverpool reveals a rented property that could be a fire trap for its unsuspecting tenants(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A static caravan is bought for the plot of land it stands on in Dunkirk, Kent. Meanwhile, a semi in Olton in the Midlands and a terrace in South Norwood in London are also sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new owners to hear plans for their buys(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up three years on with Grimsby entertainers Rob and Victoria Underwood, who sampled life on Australia's Gold Coast for a week despite Rob's misgivings about moving there permanently(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara are thrown together when a chance encounter threatens their world. Emma and Jasmine meet a woman willing to do anything for love(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Bereaved mother Jo befriends a young homeless man, a ringer for her son, who died nine months before. As she tries to get to the bottom of his story, James opens up and she offers to put him up for the night. However, when Jo's husband Patrick finds out, he tells her to send James packing. Against Patrick's wishes, Jo then walks the streets looking for James. Drama, starring Neve McIntosh, Will R Smith and Alan McKenna(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is tasked with a property-searching mission in the Forest of Dean, helping a house-hunting couple with a £475,000 budget find a home to accommodate three generations of their family. Along the way, Jonnie ventures underground to explore the area's rich ochre-mining heritage and learns more about how this valuable pigment is still in demand today(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sinead's love of cooking came when grandmother Jean taught her as a little girl. However, as she's travelled around the world her tastes have got more adventurous. Cooking against the clock, they are paired up with chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid and produce dishes from around the world on a budget of £5, from squid tempura to pad Thai and Korean chicken wings. Plus, the 10-minute challenge throws up cornflakes and ginger nut biscuits. Hosted by Rylan Clarke-Neal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Ahead of Wales' clash with England, Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by Triple Grand Slam Winner Adam Jones, host of DIY SOS Nick Knowles and Strictly Come Dancing's Amy Dowden(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six contestants cook their Signature Dish and after tasting all plates, John Torode and Gregg Wallace pick their two favourites and those cooks earn themselves a MasterChef apron and place in the next round. The remaining four take the Three Ingredients Challenge, where they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish showcasing three specific ingredients - apple, almonds and vanilla. After that there is one more challenge standing in their way from a quarter-final place. Here, the contestants' fate is in the hands of former participants Alex Rushmer, Dr Tim Kinnaird and Dhruv Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley grows suspicious of Suki's behaviour and speaks to Jean, leading to an argument. programme.desc: Barry-born make-up entrepreneurs Hannah and Sophie have a photoshoot in Ibiza, while Gonth makes a new friend who shares his passion for fast cars. In Llanelli, 17-year-old Cameron is making a mint out of creepy crawlies(n)

programme.desc: Ahead of Wales' clash with England, Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by Triple Grand Slam Winner Adam Jones, host of DIY SOS Nick Knowles and Strictly Come Dancing's Amy Dowden(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks from Abersoch to Pwllheli on the Llyn Peninsula, finding some calming beach yoga a good antidote to a testing cliff climb. Derek discovers hoof-prints made in a prehistoric forest millions of years ago, but only recently revealed in shifting sands and stops off at a special retirement village where a little bit of Poland has been re-created in north Wales(n)

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield and Redditch to help single mum Holly and Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby is back at work but she is losing patience with her mother, Lavender, and has to choose between her career and parenthood. programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)

Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Drama set in a fictional world where black people rule over a white underclass, following two young people who are divided by their race but united by love. Callum McGregor is angered when his friend is attacked by police officers during a night out but his parents tell him to do nothing, otherwise he may jeopardise his entry into an elite military academy. But an unexpected reunion with old school friend Sephy, a member of the ruling class, ignites a spark that both of them find difficult to ignore. Masali Baduza and Jack Rowan star, with Helen Baxendale, Paterson Joseph and Shaun Dingwall. Based on the novel by Malorie Blackman(n)

The remaining four must come up with a dish using three specific ingredients, after which two cooks are sent home - leaving the remaining four to cook two courses for 2016 MasterChef champion Jane Devonshire and finalists Billy Wright and Jack Layer(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Drama set in a fictional world where black people rule over a white underclass, following two young people who are divided by their race but united by love. Callum McGregor is angered when his friend is attacked by police officers during a night out but his parents tell him to do nothing, otherwise he may jeopardise his entry into an elite military academy. But an unexpected reunion with old school friend Sephy, a member of the ruling class, ignites a spark that both of them find difficult to ignore. Masali Baduza and Jack Rowan star, with Helen Baxendale, Paterson Joseph and Shaun Dingwall. Based on the novel by Malorie Blackman(n)

programme.title: The View
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers presents a review of the week's political news, including reports on the latest events at Stormont and Westminster. Plus, comment and analysis with studio guests(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Tunbridge Wells, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates a Surrey property overwhelmed with rubbish and talks to the owner about their struggles with hoarding. In west London, probate genealogist Richard tracks down a deceased homeowner's relatives to help them claim their inheritance. Plus, a housing inspection in Liverpool reveals a rented property that could be a fire trap for its unsuspecting tenants(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A static caravan is bought for the plot of land it stands on in Dunkirk, Kent. Meanwhile, a semi in Olton in the Midlands and a terrace in South Norwood in London are also sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander meet the new owners to hear plans for their buys(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up three years on with Grimsby entertainers Rob and Victoria Underwood, who sampled life on Australia's Gold Coast for a week despite Rob's misgivings about moving there permanently(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: A badly injured climber is dangling off a cliff and needs to be saved by the Mountain Rescue team. In Wales, a young boy's missing dog is found by a dog rescuer with a drone. Narrated by Kevin Duala(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool Antique Centre. Host Christina Trevanion finds out about naturalist and painter John James Audubon's Birds of America books, which are on display in the city's Central Library(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel and Zara are thrown together when a chance encounter threatens their world. Emma and Jasmine meet a woman willing to do anything for love(n)

programme.title: Moving On
programme.desc: Bereaved mother Jo befriends a young homeless man, a ringer for her son, who died nine months before. As she tries to get to the bottom of his story, James opens up and she offers to put him up for the night. However, when Jo's husband Patrick finds out, he tells her to send James packing. Against Patrick's wishes, Jo then walks the streets looking for James. Drama, starring Neve McIntosh, Will R Smith and Alan McKenna(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is tasked with a property-searching mission in the Forest of Dean, helping a house-hunting couple with a £475,000 budget find a home to accommodate three generations of their family. Along the way, Jonnie ventures underground to explore the area's rich ochre-mining heritage and learns more about how this valuable pigment is still in demand today(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore turns to upcycler Jacqui Joseph to transform a pair of chairs and a laundry basket, while designer-maker Daniel Heath hopes to give a small melamine dining table a stylish update. A pair of French armoires give Sarah herself an exciting new challenge(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Sinead's love of cooking came when grandmother Jean taught her as a little girl. However, as she's travelled around the world her tastes have got more adventurous. Cooking against the clock, they are paired up with chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid and produce dishes from around the world on a budget of £5, from squid tempura to pad Thai and Korean chicken wings. Plus, the 10-minute challenge throws up cornflakes and ginger nut biscuits. Hosted by Rylan Clarke-Neal(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances, presented by Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six contestants cook their Signature Dish and after tasting all plates, John Torode and Gregg Wallace pick their two favourites and those cooks earn themselves a MasterChef apron and place in the next round. The remaining four take the Three Ingredients Challenge, where they are tasked with creating one exceptional dish showcasing three specific ingredients - apple, almonds and vanilla. After that there is one more challenge standing in their way from a quarter-final place. Here, the contestants' fate is in the hands of former participants Alex Rushmer, Dr Tim Kinnaird and Dhruv Baker(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Shirley grows suspicious of Suki's behaviour and speaks to Jean, leading to an argument. As Gray and Kush set off for court, the solicitor subtly seeds doubts in his client's mind about the plea. Then, as Whitney's bail hearing arrives, Gray feels the pressure to deliver. But as tensions boil over, Whitney struggles to keep her cool(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final sees the six talented heat winners return to fight for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task, cooking to a brief set by columnist, broadcaster and author Grace Dent, who asks them to produce a party-sharing plate. Hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace and narrated by India Fisher(n)

programme.title: Soft Border Patrol
programme.desc: The coastal patrol tries to get Michael to come clean when its officers catch him smuggling illicit washing powder. Niall Sweeney's feasibility study into the possible bridge between Northern Ireland and Scotland includes some explosive revelations for visiting Scottish politician Erskine Forbes, while Ben and Kris learn not all acronyms are intimidating. Comedy, starring Karen Hassan, Neil Delamere, Ben McGregor, Michael Condron and Chris Patrick-Simpson(n)

programme.title: Greg James' Sport Relief Heroes
programme.desc: A defining feature of Sport Relief is the willingness of celebrities to undertake gruelling challenges in order to inspire the public to donate and get involved with the cause. Greg looks back with celebs including David Walliams, Eddie Izzard, Davina McCall, John Bishop and Zoe Ball, all of whom have put themselves to the test and beyond for the charity, finding out what motivated them(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as netball star Eboni Usoro-Brown and stand-up Darren Harriott take on comedian Susan Calman and Strictly Professional Dancer Johannes Radebe(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Glynn Purnell is joined by chefs Tom Kitchin and Georgina Hayden, and Sir Chris Hoy faces his food heaven or hell. Plus, expert Olly Smith picks wines to go with the studio dishes(n)

programme.title: Gareth Thomas' Tour de Trophy for Sport Relief
programme.desc: Rugby star Gareth Thomas embarks on a seven day, 500-mile bike ride from Cardiff to Aberdeen, towing the 40kg BBC Sports Personality of the Year trophy for Sport Relief. Gareth allows cameras record every minute of his struggle, in the hope that this film would help change attitudes to people either living in isolation or burdened by a social stigma, issues close to Gareth's heart(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Arsenal v West Ham United. Plus, news from the lunchtime game between Liverpool and Bournemouth(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the culinary duel in which two chefs create a meal in 20 minutes using ingredients brought in by the contestants helping them. In the first edition, Rylan unveils the new kitchen and introduces chefs Anna Haugh and Mike Reid, who are joined by Falkirk couple Gemma and Brian(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich visit Mansfield and Redditch to help single mum Holly and Annette, who moved into her home with her three sons two years ago(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore enlists carpenter Bruce Faulseit to repurpose an old set of coat hooks, while woodworker Norman Wilkinson tackles two chairs in need of a makeover, an creative duo Josh and Oli have the unusual task of reimagining a collection of fishing rods(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team takes on a much-loved landscape hanging together by a few threads, a treasured clockwork car and a porcelain dog damaged by a real canine(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys tests four more celebrities in the famous black chair. Taking part tonight are TV presenter Radzi Chinyanganya, actress Ruth Madeley, politician Ann Widdecombe and TV personality Tom Read Wilson, who answer questions on Olympic sprinters, Will and Grace, The Escape of King Charles II and My Fair Lady(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the final of the dancing contest. Just four performers remain in the contest, and with the help of the dance coaches Cheryl, Matthew Morrison, Oti Mabuse and Todrick Hall, they will put on one last show to impress the studio audience and win £50,000 and a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing. Who will become The Greatest Dancer? Plus, another world class guest artist performs a unique collaboration(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Contestants include Prue Leith, Brian Turner, Grace Dent, William Sitwell, Hala El-Shafie, Ed Baines, Michael Caines and Anna Haugh(n)

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a celebrity special edition of the family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as cricketer Graeme Swann and actress Rachel Parris take on Olympic hockey player Sam Quek and comedian Mark Watson(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ruby is back at work but she is losing patience with her mother, Lavender, and has to choose between her career and parenthood. Elsewhere, Megan offers Archie a job with her in Sudan, but she is not sure if she wants to leave, and Jade finally loses her patience with Marty when he is late for another shift(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents action from the latest Premier League fixtures, including Liverpool v Bournemouth at Anfield and Arsenal v West Ham United. The Reds were expected to move a step closer to the title with victory over the Cherries, while Arsenal were aiming to complete a double over their London rivals after beating them 3-1 at London Stadium in December. Plus, Crystal Palace v Watford, Sheffield United v Norwich City, Southampton v Newcastle United, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Brighton & Hove Albion and Burnley v Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Foxcatcher (2014)
programme.desc: A reclusive, eccentric millionaire persuades an Olympic gold-medal-winning wrestler to join his personal team. Initially flattered by the attention lavished upon him by his wealthy new friend, the grappler flourishes - but tension soon grows between them when his brother is employed as a coach, a situation that leads to tragedy. Fact-based drama, starring Steve Carell, Channing Tatum and Mark Ruffalo(n)

programme.title: Bailtean Fraoich
programme.desc: Heather Dewar explores the Black Isle, north of Inverness. Although not an actual island, it is almost entirely surrounded by water and despite its proximity to the capital of the Highlands, feels remote and rural. Heather learns about the peninsula's greatest claim to fame - its connection with the Brahan Seer, the legendary 17th-century figure whose prophetic powers eventually led to his grisly death. Heather, a keen artist, shows that her creative skills do not extend to crafting when she attempts to help Mairi Rhind spin some yarn(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A murder at a remote cottage in Northumberland takes DCI Vera Stanhope back to a place full of childhood memories and reminds her of an unsolved case from earlier in her career. Delving into the local politics surrounding a controversial proposal to dig a quarry, she and her team find motives in the unlikeliest of places - until they realise that the truth could be hidden within the land itself. Detective drama, adapted from Ann Cleeves' novel The Crow Trap, guest starring Daniela Nardini (This Life) alongside Brenda Blethyn(n)

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Mystery drama set in Bath, where the wildly ambitious DCI McDonald finds herself paired with the shy, modest DS Dodds, who has languished on the shelf for most of his working life. However, it soon turns out the unassuming sergeant has a hidden talent for puzzle solving. Their first case sees the pair looking into the shooting of a homeless man in the deserted mansion of local industrialist Max Crockett. The Crockett appear to be a close-knit group but it soon becomes clear they have secrets to hide. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star as the detectives, with a guest appearance from Robert Lindsay(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Wales (Kick-off 4.45pm). All the action from both teams' penultimate match of the championship, held at Twickenham. England kept their title hopes alive with a 24-12 victory over Ireland last time out, while last year's Grand Slam-winners Wales look set to relinquish their crown after suffering back-to-back defeats against the Irish and France. Mark Pougatch presents, with analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson, Gareth Thomas and Mike Phillips, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Shane Williams and Lawrence Dallaglio(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Jax Jones and Ella Henderson perform live at the start of this week's entertainment extravaganza, while the hosts face a dance battle with Britain's Got Talent: The Champions winners Twist & Pulse in the End of the Show Show. Plus, Stephen Mulhern returns for Ant vs Dec Goes Global, one lucky audience member plays to Win the Ads and the Men in Brown continue on their mission to track down which mystery celebrity has The Squits. More deserving audience members and viewers at home also win a Place on the Plane(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the third and final episode of the Battle rounds. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition, but those that aren't so lucky can still be stolen by another coach(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals. This time, Martin's GMC report requires he passes three refresher courses which will then decide whether he is deemed fit to practice, starting with phlebotomy. Meanwhile, Louisa focuses on her new career as a child and adolescent counsellor. Much to Bert's dismay, Al hires Caitlin, and confesses to his accidental proposal(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: The duo look into the murder of an eight-year-old boy, coming up against a paedophile who cruelly deceives his victim's family. Dom's HIV test comes back negative, so he sets out to celebrate - only to endanger his own life and jeopardise Rachel's sergeant's exam in the process. Janet tells Andy she is having second thoughts about their brief liaison, but he finds her decision hard to accept. Suranne Jones and Lesley Sharp star, with Liam Boyle, Nicholas Gleaves and Joe Duttine(n)

Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Barlows and Platts convene for Adam and Sarah's wedding. Tyrone and Fiz unite against Jade. Gary bumps into an old acquaintance(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the deaths of a female pensioner and a vicar who were both killed during Broughton village's annual contest between men and women. The pair soon discover the preacher had a secret that could provide a motive - but when another body is found, they realise the answer to all the deaths lies in the history books. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Winter and Barnaby investigate when Lower Pampling's star cricketer Leo Henderson is found dead after a match in the grounds, pummelled to death by cricket balls. Barnaby is surprised to see DS Jones undercover as one of Leo's team-mates, and his former colleague reveals he is working on suspected cases of match-fixing in the area, but things take an even more sinister turn when captain Fitz Theara is stabbed through the heart with a cricket stump(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover a dead body surrounded by rabbits that have escaped from a local pet show, after someone deliberately freed them from their cages. At the crime scene, DCI Barnaby and DS Winter discover that the victim died from a stab wound to the neck, and theorise that the murderer might be a competitor sabotaging the Belville Small Pet Show, and question the Best in Show defending champion Timothy Benson. The pair must work out if this is a sign of rivalry taken too far, or is the killer driven by something darker?(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: The season of goodwill descends on the Larkin household, and the family celebrates the 'perfick' Christmas. Drama, based on the characters created by HE Bates, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Another chance to see John Thaw's farewell appearance as Colin Dexter's Oxford detective. programme.desc: Arthur celebrates a reunion with old army pal Yorkie. Brian Glover guest stars, with George Cole and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is shot by an intruder in his flat, and enters a near coma. Cowley fears a terrorist group - two of whose members were blown up during a recent car chase by CI5 - is responsible. While Doyle experiences surreal dreams involving a ghostly graveyard, Cowley and Bodie attempt to pursue the villain behind the attack. Action drama, with Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A trusted informant tips Regan off about an upcoming robbery, but the gang involved kidnaps his daughter to blackmail him into taking no action. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The suspicious death of a Holocaust survivor presents Quincy with a fresh mystery to solve, and sets him on the trail of a Nazi war criminal determined to evade capture(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A gypsy tries to thwart Theo's pursuit of the serial killer who murdered several members of her clan, as she intends to carry out her own lethal brand of vengeance(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Simon Templar is suspected of involvement in a series of jewel robberies on the French Riviera, and has to stay one step ahead of the police long enough to catch the real culprit. Guest starring Ronnie Barker and Aimee Delamain(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur loses a fortune in cash when his wine supplier is mugged and the money he entrusted is stolen. To add insult to injury the dodgy entrepreneur becomes the prime suspect for the attack, leading him and Terry on a mission to prove his innocence. George Cole stars(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are assigned to prompt Arab cultural attache and assassin Rahad into carrying out a murderous deed, in the hope they can catch him red-handed just before he commits the act and force him out of the country. Comedy actor John Junkin guests stars alongside Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of robberies is committed on affluent couples travelling down a country lane, and it transpires two of the suspected offenders are wealthy themselves. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Sam Quek, Basil Brush, Andi Peters and Charlie Higson pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. programme.desc: Jacqui Oatley presents coverage of the evening session on day two at the Butlin's Minehead Resort, as the event nicknamed the FA Cup of Darts - due to the random nature of the draw - continues with sixth-round matches. Eventual champion Nathan Aspinall recorded a victory over Steve Lennon at this stage last year, while Dave Chisnall, James Wade and Simon Whitlock all suffered shock defeats in round six, losing to Simon Stevenson, Ross Smith and Josh Payne respectively. programme.desc: As Britain has grown into a nation of coffee lovers, Starbucks and Nespresso have become two of the world's biggest coffee brands. They have revolutionised what people drink and how they consume it. On the back of their success, both firms make bold claims about how their beans are ethically sourced and, in particular, that child labour is not used in their supply chains. But in this investigation, Dispatches travelled to Guatemala and discovered young children working long hours in gruelling conditions to pick coffee beans that supply these two coffee giants. Reporter Antony Barnett hears how a day's work can earn the children little more than the price of a Starbucks latte or a pack of Nespresso pods(n)

This engine, a sister to the Mallard, the fastest steam loco ever built, is due to be retired, so this will be one of the last opportunities to see it.
